There is a LOT of things in kubejs that NEED to be in the right folders. You could have all your code correct and nothing will work. Things like recipe scripts actual game function changes functions on player join command creation etc Lets start off by learing where you can find your mod files and kubejs files The biggest thing you need to know is how to find your game files. For me im going to show this in CurseForge but it may differ from launcher to launcher Lets start by actually going into your modpack in CurseForge and right next to the play button you will see 3 little dots Now click on open folder

Now that we are in your modpack directory you need to find a folder called Kubejs, that is where ALL of your work will be done Inside the Kubejs folder you will see a few different folders and a ReadMe file, the few files you will more than likely be worried about will be the server_scripts and startup_scripts. This will be where most of your code will be ran, made, or messed with Both the Server_Script and the startup_scripts folder will usually have a file in them by default. In different version they may be names something different but in this example they are called main.js in the startup_scripts and main.js in the server_scripts but are sometimes names example.js

To start making your own file or script, it is very simple. You can start out by using the example one that is created for you at first launch but when you are ready to create a NEW file it is very simple. Start out by going into one of the two folders that we spoke about before (startup_scripts/Server_scripts) Start out by right clicking anywhere in the folder and go down to NEW then click on new text document Once you get a new file, rename it to something relative to what you are changing and change the .txt to .js When clicking ENTER it will ask you if you are sure you would like to change the file type from .txt to .js, just click yes

When coding in kubejs or any coding related things for minecraft you are going to need to know the namespaces of items, blocks, machines, fluids, etc. To easier find them when you are in game Press F3 + H to enable advanced tool tips in game and Now when you hover over any block, item, fluid, etc you will see a new name show up looking like minecraft:oak_log instead of just oak_log these are the names you will be using in code to tell the game what items you are wanting to use. Examples are here of before and after the advanced tool tips are enabled

In a main code/script file we will start with basics of coding. The main line of code that all scripts need when creating a new recipe or changing a recipe is a ServerEvents.recipes(event => { This line tells KubeJS: “Run this code when Minecraft is loading or updating recipes.”:
ServerEvents → This is a built-in KubeJS system. It means “We are listening for server-side game events.”
recipes blocks items loot tables
documents.s: The server is the part of Minecraft that controls gameplay logic like:

documents.t: .recipes → This tells KubeJS exactly what event we care about “We only want to modify crafting recipes.” So this line means “When Minecraft is handling recipes…”

This is EXAMPLE 1 (example is in neoforge 1.21.1) Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shapeless('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'minecraft:dirt', 'minecraft:dirt' ]) }) Loading...
documents.s: Create your first ITEM recipe (in server_scripts) NeoForge 1.21.1

documents.t: This is EXAMPLE 2 (example is in forge 1.20.1) Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shapeless('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'minecraft:dirt', 'minecraft:dirt' ]) }) Loading...
documents.s: Create your first ITEM recipe (in server_scripts) FORGE 1.20.1

documents.t: This is EXAMPLE 3 (example is in fabric 1.20.1) WARNING Server_Script dont show up until after the first world is loaded in this version of fabric Create a file at :\Instances\Mod pack\kubejs\server_scripts: grass.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shapeless('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'minecraft:dirt', 'minecraft:dirt' ]) }) Loading...
documents.s: Create your first ITEM recipe (in server_scripts) Fabric 1.20.1

documents.t: ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:grass_block', [ 'DD ', ' ', ' ' ], { D: 'minecraft:dirt' }) }) Loading... To make a shaped recipe it is pretty much just like the shapless just with a few extra steps. The biggest thing is event.shaped('minecraft:grass_block', [ This tells minecraft that it is a shaped recipe, and sense there is event.shaped instead event.custom it automatically uses the crafting table. the code part that looks like 'DD ', ' ', ' ' is just telling the game what shape to put the items in the crafting table. Look at spaces inbeteen the '' and think of thoes as just empty spots in the crafting table This is what we call "Keys" This just gives a name of something to a letter. What ever item you give to this letter is what will be used in the crafting table!!! { D: 'minecraft:dirt' })

There is many different ways you can craft things using chaped crafting, here are three more examples of some fun things to doc // Vertical Recipe Example ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:diamond', [ 'I ', 'I ', 'I ' ], { I: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) }) Loading... // Corner Recipe Example ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:gold_ingot', [ 'G ', ' G ', ' G' ], { G: 'minecraft:gold_nugget' }) }) Loading... // Full Square Recipe Example ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.shaped('minecraft:emerald', [ 'EEE', 'EEE', 'EEE' ], { E: 'minecraft:emerald_block' }) }) Loading...

Removing unwanted recipes is very common in modpacks. All removal scripts go in the kubejs/server_scripts/ folder. // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.remove// Your removal code goes here }); This is specifically what you need to remove recipes in bulk, sings, by tags, and so on. Check the lower examples to see more information event.remove

Example 1, Removing the output of a item being crafted // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all ways to craft a Diamond Pickaxe event.remove({ output: 'minecraft:diamond_pickaxe' }) }) Loading... Example 2, same as example 1 just with a different item // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all ways to craft a Diamond Pickaxe event.remove({ output: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) }) Loading...

Example 3, This is how you can remove the outputs or recipes of EVERY item, block, or tool that is in a specific mod. This example shows you it working with the Create mod. // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove ALL recipes from the Create mod event.remove({ mod: "create" }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Removing ALL recipes in ANY mod

documents.t: Example 4, This is how you remove a recipe if it uses a specific item or material as its input or ingredient, This example shows me removing any recipe that uses oak planks as an ingredient, so every block or item in the game that uses oak planks no longer have a crafting recipe // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove any recipe that uses Oak Planks as an ingredient event.remove({ input: 'minecraft:oak_planks' }); }) Loading...

Example 5, This specific code shows you how to remove the output recipes of a entire block. in this case it shows you that if you do this with the campfire then you will no longer be allowed to put anything food wise on the camp fire and cook it. It completely removes cooking on the campfire // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all campfire cooking recipes event.remove({ type: 'minecraft:campfire_cooking' }); }) Loading...
documents.s: Removing ALL types of output for a specific block (the campfire outputs)

documents.t: Example 5, this example shows that you can remove specific outputs also and not just ALL outputs. this shows that you can disable or remove smelting a ore or raw ore like raw iron in a furnace but still have it working in ONLY the blast furnace AND this also works for the camp fire , // File: server_scripts/remove_recipes.js // Remove all blasting recipes for iron ServerEvents.recipes(event => { // Remove all campfire cooking recipes event.remove({ type: 'minecraft:smelting', output: 'minecraft:iron_ingot' }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Removing SPECIF types of output for a specific block (the furnace outputs and blast furnace outputs)

documents.t: There is a few things in the default Create mod you can modify with kubjs and its all very starter friendly. Lets look at some examples of things EVERYONE changes

mechanical crafters Millstone Deployers Including sequenced assembly deploying stripping automative crafting Mechanical Saw wood cutting crafting stone cutting glass cutting crafting Item Drain Draining items into fluids and items Mechanical Mixer automated brewing automatic shapless crafting Muxing (crafting) Including heated and non heated recipes Mechanical Press Automated Packing (ITEMS - shaped crafting) Compacting (auto crafting W Items/Fluids) Pressing (things like) -iron sheets dirt into path blocks etc. Encased Fans Including haunting (soul campfire and soul fire) smoking (camp fire and fire) washing blasting and sand paper

This may look like a LOT of reading, but i promis this will be worth it. We will show you an example of everything here. There should also be different versions of each example of the code to work on multiple different versions sense loads of things change between FORGE, NEOFORGE, and fabric. So be sure to reach wha version this code is for before trying it.

forge 1.20.1 In this example we show you how to make a very unfair recipe, but fun none the less. You need to fill up a 4X4 set of mechanical crafters to make 1 single oak plank. Very annoying but this is a great recipe mechanical_crafters.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "create:mechanical_crafting", pattern: [ "AAAA", "AAAA", "AAAA", "AAAA" ], key: { A: { item: "minecraft:oak_log" } }, result: { item: "minecraft:oak_planks", count: 1 } }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 this recipe shows you how to have a input to the millestone turn into something as the output. this one specifically shows you putting 1 log into the millestone and getting a plank out as the output Millstone.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:milling", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "chance": 1 } ], "processingTime": 1 }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 this recipe shows you how to have 1 item as the input (deployer holding) and another item on the belt and how to get a output from all of that. This shows you how to take a Log being held, deploying it onto another Log and getting a plank as the output deploying.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:deploying", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ] }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe shows you how to take a input such as oak_logs and making a output of stone. The output and input can be most items and blocks you want Mechanical_saw.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:cutting", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:stone", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 50 }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item IN to the drain and output two things, You absolutly have to have a fluid output. if you want 2 items the second one has to be a item, not another fluid. The drain can only hold up to one fluid at a time. Just make sure you 100% of the time have some sort of fluid coming out as a result or you will crash your game and the recipe wont work drain.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "create:emptying", ingredients: [ { item: "minecraft:wheat" } ], results: [ { item: "minecraft:diamond" }, { fluid: "minecraft:water", amount: 1000 } ] }) }) Loading...
## Mechanical Mixing (`Non heated`) **forge 1.20.1** ![normal mixing](/img/c/mechanical_mixing.png) ![normal mixing](/img/c/mixingJEI.png) *This recipe will show you how to take a item INTO a basin and mix it with the mechanical mixer `non heated` for a specific output, this one shows you how to put in `oak_logs` and get `oak_planks` as a result* ```jsx title="mixing.js" ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 100 }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item INTO a basin and mix it with the mechanical mixer heated for a specific output, this one shows you how to put in oak_planks and get netherrack as a result heated_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:netherrack", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 100, "heatRequirement": "heated" }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to take a item INTO a basin and mix it with the mechanical mixer SUPER heated for a specific output, this one shows you how to put in oak_planks and get netherrack as a result super_heated_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:netherrack", "count": 1 } ], "processingTime": 100, "heatRequirement": "superheated" }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to mix two different fluids into a different fluid as your output fluid_to_fluid_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "fluid": "minecraft:water", "amount": 1000 //this is the amount of fluid in buckets, 1000 means 1000mb and 1000mb means 1 bucket. 500mb means half a bucket }, { "fluid": "create:chocolate", "amount": 1000 } ], "results": [ { "fluid": "create:honey", "amount": 1000 } ], "processingTime": 100 }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Mechanical Mixing (fluid + fluid to fluid mixing)

documents.t: forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to mix a fluid and a item into a different fluid as your output fluid_and_item_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:mixing", "ingredients": [ { "fluid": "minecraft:water", "amount": 1000 }, { "item": "create:bar_of_chocolate" } ], "results": [ { "fluid": "create:honey", "amount": 1000 } ], "processingTime": 100 }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Mechanical Mixing (fluid + Item to fluid mixing)

documents.t: *now its time to learn the one things that people LOVE and also get confused on. Encased Fans can be amazing to use for many different reasons BUT the name spacing for different types of things can confuse people. So here are a few recipe types you can do with these haunting create:haunting washing create:splashing Smoking (Fan + Fire) create:smoking Blasting (Fan + Lava) create:blasting

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do haunting and this is by putting a soul fire or soul camp fire infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows Oak_logs going in and oak_planks coming out haunting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:haunting", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ] }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do smoking/cooking and this is by putting a fire or camp fire infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows Oak_logs going in and oak_planks coming out smoking.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.smoking( 'minecraft:oak_planks', 'minecraft:oak_log' ) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do Washing and this is by putting water infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows Oak_logs going in and oak_planks coming out washing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:splashing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 1 } ] }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do blasting and this is by putting lava infront of the encased Fans and getting a output of some sort. this example shows smooth_stone going in and oak_planks coming out bulk_blasting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.blasting( 'minecraft:oak_planks', 'minecraft:smooth_stone' ) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do sanding and this is rubbing something against sandpaper to get a output In this example you see oak_logs being sanded into oak_planks bulk_blasting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:sandpaper_polishing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks" } ] }) }) Loading...

forge 1.20.1 This recipe will show you how to do crushing and this when you have two crushing wheels spinning inwards very quick and put two items in and get some items out. This is typically used for automatic ore creation or duplication bulk_blasting.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "create:crushing", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_log" } ], "results": [ { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 4 }, { "item": "minecraft:oak_planks", "count": 2, "chance": 0.5 } ], "processingTime": 150 }) }) Loading...
Lets show you how to use kubejs to add your own custom recipes with the Farmers delight blocks, pots, and more!
documents.s: Learn to use Kubejs to work with farmers delight

documents.t: forge 1.20.1 For creating stuff for the Pot You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for Carrots Potatos and a bowl as our input and a diamond as the output cooking_on_a_pot.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "farmersdelight:cooking", "recipe_book_tab": "meals", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:potato" }, { "item": "minecraft:carrot" }, { "item": "minecraft:bowl" } ], "result": { "item": "minecraft:diamond" }, "experience": 1.0, "cookingtime": 200 }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the POT

documents.t: forge 1.20.1 For creating stuff with the stove you want your input and output, for this example we have a apple going in and a golden_apple coming out. The Stove in Farmers Delight does not have its own recipe functions, it works off of the base minecraft campfire recipe. Anything you can cook on a campfire can be cooked on the stove. This means you need to do minecraft:campfire_cooking to register the recipe cooking_on_a_stove.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "minecraft:campfire_cooking", "ingredient": { "item": "minecraft:apple" }, "result": "minecraft:golden_apple", "experience": 0.35, "cookingtime": 50 }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the STOVE

documents.t: forge 1.20.1 For creating stuff with the cutting board you want your input and output, for this example we have a coal going in & diamonds, and sand coming out. This biggest part here is to use "type": "farmersdelight:cutting" to register your recipe for the cutting board cutting_board.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "farmersdelight:cutting", "ingredients": [ { "item": "minecraft:coal" } ], "result": [ { "item": "minecraft:diamond" }, { "item": "minecraft:sand" } ], "sound": "minecraft:item.axe.strip", "tool": [ { "type": "farmersdelight:tool_action", "action": "axe_strip" }, { "tag": "minecraft:axes" } ] }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the cutting board

documents.t: forge 1.20.1 For creating stuff with the Skillet is the EXACT same as the STOVE you have to add nothing else to your scrip, if it works on the stove it works with the skillet Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Skillet

documents.t: (check next section for addon custom code) Lets show you how to use kubejs to add your own custom recipes with AE2 this section will include the inscriber and the charger
documents.s: Learn to use Kubejs to work with AE2 with NO addons

documents.t: For creating stuff for the inscriber You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for printed_calculation_circuit apples and a printed_logic_circuit as our input and a golden_apple as the output This one is a little more differnt then most recipes you have been learning lately through out many kubejs tutorials Most code dont have a bottom top and middle section you have to chose from. But with this block specifically them locations just mean what slot you are wanting. So make sure to really check your recipes when changing them around The biggest thing is here is the "type": "ae2:inscriber" inscriber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "ae2:inscriber", "ingredients": { "bottom": { "item": "ae2:printed_logic_processor" }, "middle": { "item": "minecraft:apple" }, "top": { "item": "ae2:printed_calculation_processor" } }, "mode": "press", "result": { "item": "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Inscriber

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Charger You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for a apple and a golden_apple as the output This one is a little more simple. Just a input and a output. The biggest thing is here is the "type": "ae2:charger" charger.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "ae2:charger", "ingredient": { "item": "minecraft:apple" }, "result": { "item": "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Charger

documents.t: Lets show you how to use kubejs to add your own custom recipes with ExtendedAE this section will include the circuit_cutter, the Extended_inscribers, and extended_charger
documents.s: Learn to use Kubejs to work with ExtendedAE

documents.t: For creating stuff for the circuit_cutter You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apple and water as our input and a golden_apple as the output *This recipe is a little more different than what most are used to ExtendedAE has there own way of coding in liquid, items and so on. This one specifically want a fluid_input instead of it just being "fluid": minecraft:water Same thing for items. Normally you would see "item" :minecraft:apple but in here they want it to be "item_input": So just keep that in mind later when adding more code* Extended_ae_slicer.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "expatternprovider:circuit_cutter", "fluid_input": { "amount": 100, "ingredient": { "fluid": "minecraft:water" } }, "item_input": { "amount": 1, "ingredient": { "item": "minecraft:apple" } }, "output": { "count": 1, "item": "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the circuit_cutter

documents.t: So if you have seen the LearnAE2 part of this page you will already know what the inscriber and charger is. The cool thing about the extendedAE addon is it uses the recipes from the base inscriber and charger so you dont have to do Anything different!! inscriber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "ae2:inscriber", "ingredients": { "bottom": { "item": "ae2:printed_logic_processor" }, "middle": { "item": "minecraft:apple" }, "top": { "item": "ae2:printed_calculation_processor" } }, "mode": "press", "result": { "item": "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading... charger.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "ae2:charger", "ingredient": { "item": "minecraft:apple" }, "result": { "item": "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Extended_Charger and Extended_inscriber

documents.t: (check next section for addon custom code) Lets show you how to use kubejs to add your own custom recipes with Mekanism this section will include the Energized Smelter, enriching_factory
documents.s: Learn to use Kubejs to work with Mekanism (no addons)

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Energized Smelter You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples as our input and a golden_apple as the output The Smelting factory types all run off of whats already in minecraft If it can smelt in a furnace it can smelt in the factories. So nothing to crazy. Just use a normal smelting recipe in your code Energized Smelter.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "minecraft:smelting", "ingredient": { "item": "minecraft:apple" }, "result": "minecraft:golden_apple", "experience": 0.35, }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Energized Smelter

documents.t: For creating stuff for the enrichment Chamber You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples as our input and a golden_apple as the output enrichment Chamber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:enriching", "input":{"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output": {"item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the enrichment Chamber

documents.t: For creating stuff for the crusher You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples as our input and a golden_apple as the output crusher.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:crushing", "input": {"ingredient":{"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output": {"count":2, "item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the crusher

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Combiner You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples and Gold nugget as our input and a golden_apple as the output Combiner.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:combining", "extraInput": {"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:gold_nugget"}}, "mainInput": {"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output": {"item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Combiner

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Osmium Compressor You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples and nuclear_waste as our input and a golden_apple as the output Osmium Compressor.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:compressing", "chemicalInput": {"amount":1, "gas":"mekanism:nuclear_waste"}, "itemInput":{"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output": {"item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Osmium Compressor

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Energized Smelter You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples and Power as our input and a golden_apple as the output This uses the default smelting code used in minecraft. if its smeltable, its usable in this block. so make a normal smelting recipe (the first recipe on this page)
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Energized Smelter

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Metalluric_infuser You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples bio as our input and a golden_apple as the output metallurgic_infusing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:metallurgic_infusing", "chemicalInput": {"amount":40, "tag":"mekanism:bio"}, "itemInput": {"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output": {"count":4, "item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Metalluric infuser

documents.t: For creating stuff for the Purification Chamber You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples, oxygen, and Flint as our input and a golden_apple as the output purification_chamber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:purifying", "chemicalInput": {"amount":2, "gas": "mekanism:oxygen"}, "itemInput": {"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output": {"count":18, "item": "minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Purification Chamber

documents.t: For creating stuff for Chemical Injection Chamber You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples and water vapor as our input and a golden_apple as the output Chemical Injection Chamber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:injecting", "chemicalInput": {"amount":1, "tag":"mekanism:water_vapor"}, "itemInput": {"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "output":{"item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Chemical Injection Chamber

documents.t: For creating stuff for Precision Sawmille You want to figure out what you want as your input and your output. For this one we are going for apples input and a golden_apple as the output with a chance of extra apples Precision Sawmille.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type":"mekanism:sawing", "input": {"ingredient": {"item":"minecraft:apple"}}, "mainOutput":{"count":6, "item":"minecraft:golden_apple"}, "secondaryChance":0.25, "secondaryOutput":{"item":"minecraft:golden_apple"} }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Precision Sawmille

documents.t: For creating stuff for Rotary Condensentrator You want to figure out what Gas you want as your input, and what fluid you want as your output. For this one i have the gas uranium_hexafluoride as my input and minecraft:water as my output. If you switch the two around as input and output the game will automatically change the recipe to work as Decondensentrating instead!! Rotary Condensentrator.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:rotary", gasInput: { amount: 1, gas: "mekanism:uranium_hexafluoride" }, fluidOutput: { amount: 1, fluid: "minecraft:water" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Rotary Condensentrator

documents.t: For creating stuff for Electrolytic seperator You want to figure out what Fluid you want as your input, and what fluid or gas you want as your output. For this one i have the fluid minecraft:water as my input and gas:chlorine and gas Oxygen as my output. Electrolytic_seperator.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "mekanism:separating", "input": { "amount": 2, "tag": "minecraft:water" }, "leftGasOutput": { "amount": 2, "gas": "mekanism:chlorine" }, "rightGasOutput": { "amount": 1, "gas": "mekanism:oxygen" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Electrolytic seperator

documents.t: For creating stuff for Chemical dissolution chamber You want to figure out what gas you want as your main input. Mine is sulfuric asid, and the item input is iron ore. And the output here will be a custom output of slurry this one is completely custom from Mekanism Chemical_dissolution_chamber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ "type": "mekanism:dissolution", "gasInput": { "amount": 1, "gas": "mekanism:sulfuric_acid" }, "itemInput": { "ingredient": { "item": "minecraft:iron_ore" } }, "output": { "amount": 1000, "chemicalType": "slurry", "slurry": "mekanism:dirty_copper" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Chemical dissolution chamber

documents.t: For creating stuff for Pigment Extractor You want to figure out what item you want as your main input. Mine is minecraft:golden_apple. And the output here will be a pigment from mekanism this one will be yellow Pigment_Extractor.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:pigment_extracting", input: { ingredient: { item: "minecraft:golden_apple" } }, output: { amount: 64, pigment: "mekanism:yellow" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Pigment Extractor

documents.t: For creating stuff for Painting machine You want to figure out what item you want as your main input. Mine is minecraft:apple. and my second input will be the yellow pigment. And the output here will be a golden_apple painting_machine.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:painting", chemicalInput: { amount: 256, pigment: "mekanism:yellow" }, itemInput: { ingredient: { item: "minecraft:apple" } }, output: { item: "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Painting machine

documents.t: For creating stuff for Pigment Mixing You want to figure out what 2 pigments you want as your main inputs. Mine is black and yellow. And the output here will be a green. Yeah i know it makes no sense but hey, thats the fun of custom code Pigment_mixing.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:pigment_mixing", leftInput: { amount: 1, pigment: "mekanism:black" }, rightInput: { amount: 4, pigment: "mekanism:yellow" }, output: { amount: 5, pigment: "mekanism:green" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Pigment Mixing

documents.t: For creating stuff for Chemical Washer You want to figure out what chemical you want as your main inputs. And then use water as your other main input. And the output here will be a different chemical Its up to you what all you put in here. Any chemical to be washed, and any fluid as the cleaning supply. and any chemical as the output. Chemical_Washer.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:washing", fluidInput: { amount: 5, tag: "minecraft:water" }, slurryInput: { amount: 1, slurry: "mekanism:dirty_iron" }, output: { amount: 1, slurry: "mekanism:clean_copper" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Chemical Washer

documents.t: For creating stuff for Chemical Crystallizer You want to figure out what chemical you want as your main input. and then what item you want as your output. My chemical here is dirty Osmium and my output item is golden_apple. Chemical_Crystallizer.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:crystallizing", chemicalType: "slurry", input: { amount: 200, slurry: "mekanism:dirty_osmium" }, output: { item: "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Chemical Crystallizer

documents.t: For creating stuff for Pressurized Reaction Chamber You want a fluid as your first main input mine here is lava and then you want a gas of some sort, mine here is Polonium. After that you want a item input and here it is a Apple and the output for me is golden_apple Pressurized+Reaction_Chamber.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:reaction", duration: 100, fluidInput: { amount: 1000, tag: "minecraft:lava" }, gasInput: { amount: 1000, gas: "mekanism:polonium" }, itemInput: { ingredient: { item: "minecraft:apple" } }, gasOutput: { amount: 1000, gas: "mekanism:spent_nuclear_waste" }, itemOutput: { item: "minecraft:golden_apple" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Pressurized Reaction Chamber

documents.t: For creating stuff for Isotopic Centrifuge is just putting gas in and getting gas out (DOES NOT SUPPORT LIQUIDS OR SLURRY) Isotopic_Centrifuge.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:centrifuging", input: { amount: 10, gas: "mekanism:fissile_fuel" }, output: { amount: 1, gas: "mekanism:plutonium" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Isotopic Centrifuge

documents.t: For creating stuff for Chemical Oxidizer is just putting a item in and getting a gas out Chemical_Oxidizer.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:oxidizing", input: { ingredient: { item: "minecraft:golden_apple" } }, output: { amount: 1000, gas: "mekanism:sulfur_dioxide" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Chemical Oxidizer

documents.t: For creating stuff for Chemical Infuser is just putting two Chemicals in and getting a chemical out. Chemical_Infuser.js ServerEvents.recipes(event => { event.custom({ type: "mekanism:chemical_infusing", leftInput: { amount: 1, gas: "mekanism:hydrogen" }, rightInput: { amount: 1, gas: "mekanism:chlorine" }, output: { amount: 1, gas: "mekanism:nuclear_waste" } }) }) Loading...
documents.s: Creating a Recipe for the Chemical Infuser

documents.t: YOU DO NOT NEED THESE BUT THEY ARE NICE TO HAVE loot.js version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Create version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Mekanism version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Additions version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Powah version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Delight version may depend on what version you are playing on: kube.js Ars Nouveau version may depend on what version you are playing on:
